headline: USA RARE EARTH BUYS BRAZIL'S SERRA VERDE FOR $2.8B TO CHALLENGE CHINA'S HEAVY RARE EARTH GRIP
snippet: USA Rare Earth (USAR) agreed to acquire Brazilian producer Serra Verde in a $2.8B cash-and-stock deal, positioning the company to account for over 50% of non-China heavy rare earth oxide production by 2027. The deal directly targets China's dominance of dysprosium, terbium, and yttrium supply chains critical to EV motors and defense applications.

headline: AGNICO EAGLE STRIKES C$3.4B TRIPLE DEAL TO LOCK UP FINLAND'S CENTRAL LAPLAND GOLD BELT
snippet: Agnico Eagle announced three simultaneous acquisitions — Rupert Resources (C$2.9B), Aurion Resources (~C$350M), and B2Gold's Fingold JV stake ($325M) — consolidating a 2,492 km² land package around its Kittilä mine into a targeted 500,000-oz/yr Finnish gold hub. The deals create Europe's largest single gold district under one owner, with projected synergies of up to $365M.

headline: GOLD FALLS TO $4,777, SILVER DROPS 4% AS U.S. SEIZES IRANIAN SHIP AND CEASEFIRE HANGS IN BALANCE
snippet: Spot gold eased 0.9% to $4,777/oz on Tuesday as a firmer dollar and cautious optimism around US-Iran ceasefire talks reduced safe-haven demand, while silver tumbled 3.95% to $76.55/oz — hitting a one-week low. The fragile two-week ceasefire expires imminently, but a weekend incident involving a U.S.-seized Iranian cargo ship sent oil surging 5% and added fresh uncertainty.

headline: RECORD GOLD PRICES Are Flooding Junior Exploration Budgets — Heraeus Says Safe Haven Status Intact
snippet: Precious metals refiner Heraeus says gold's safe haven credentials remain fully intact near $4,800/oz and that sustained high gold and silver prices are directly boosting exploration spending at both majors and junior miners. The report argues the current bull market has structural legs that extend well beyond short-term geopolitical risk premiums.

headline: WELLS FARGO: Debasement Trade Could Drive Gold to $8,000 — Currency Cycle 'Less Than Halfway Through'
snippet: Wells Fargo's chief equity strategist argues the global currency debasement cycle is less than halfway through its run, making $8,000/oz gold a plausible bull case by 2027 with a base case of $6,000–$6,300 for 2026. The bank says four of five macro scenarios point to continued monetary debasement, with central bank gold buying accelerating the thesis even after gold's recent pullback.

headline: STAGFLATION HAS ARRIVED: Greg Weldon on Gold, the Dollar, China, and Why the Fed Is Trapped
snippet: Veteran analyst Greg Weldon tells Money Metals that stagflation is no longer a future risk — labor force participation has collapsed to 1977 levels while inflation persists, leaving the Fed with no good exit. Weldon sees this backdrop as structurally bullish for gold and silver, with China's yuan policy adding a second, independent debasement vector driving the metals.

headline: Sunshine Silver Mining WEIGHS $400M IPO to Reopen Idaho's Historic Sunshine Mine for 2028 Production
snippet: Sunshine Silver Mining & Refining is pursuing a dual NYSE/TSX listing targeting $400M in proceeds to restart operations at the historic Sunshine Mine in Idaho's Silver Valley, with first production targeted for 2028. The project also hosts antimony, gallium, and germanium, positioning it within the U.S. critical minerals push alongside its silver core.

headline: JUDGE ORDERS 30-DAY HALT to BHP/Lundin's Vicuña Copper Project in Argentina Over Environmental Challenge
snippet: An Argentine judge in La Rioja ordered a 30-day suspension of activities at BHP and Lundin Mining's Vicuña copper project — one of the world's largest undeveloped copper-gold-silver deposits — citing absent environmental impact assessments, while provincial police physically blocked the main access road. The joint-venture company says it had not been formally notified and the operation continued.

headline: U.S. EYES CONGO'S RUBAYA COLTAN MINE as State Dept Confirms American Companies Scouting DRC Critical Assets
snippet: A State Department official confirmed U.S. companies are showing 'significant' interest in Congolese mineral assets including the rebel-held Rubaya coltan deposit — one of the world's richest tantalum sources — as part of the US-Congo minerals partnership. Any U.S. investment will require alignment with Washington-led peace efforts in eastern DRC, where Rwandan-backed forces currently control the mine.

headline: U.S. BACKS $50M Investment in South Africa Rare Earth Project Despite Phalaborwa Diplomatic Clash
snippet: The U.S. International Development Finance Corporation committed $50M equity to the Phalaborwa Rare Earths Project in South Africa, targeting extraction from two enormous industrial waste dunes at a former chemical processing plant. The investment signals Washington's determination to fund non-China rare earth supply even amid active diplomatic tensions with Pretoria.

headline: Junior Explorer COPPER INTELLIGENCE Targets Untapped Eastern Congo Copper District, Drilling in 4-6 Weeks
snippet: U.S. junior Copper Intelligence plans to begin its maiden drill program within four to six weeks at what it believes is a previously unexplored copper district in eastern Congo, geologically distinct from the established Copperbelt. The company is leveraging satellite and geochemical data to target a system it says conventional exploration methods have consistently overlooked.
